excerpt: Safety On 20 June, the Bolivian government declared a 90-day nationwide state of emergency for security forces to clear roadblocks and protests, and to stabilise the security situation. Large-scale political demonstrations, roadblocks, protests, strikes and events can occur and escalate with little warning. Violent crime, associated with drug trafficking, poses a risk in some parts of the country, particularly in the Chapare region. Petty theft is common in tourist areas, buses and bus stations. Violent crime against foreigners occurs, including armed robbery, assault, and food and drink spiking. Victims are forced to withdraw money from ATMs to secure their release.

Secure .gov websites use HTTPS lock ( ) or https:// means you’ve safely connected to the .gov website. Share sensitive information only on official, secure websites. Venezuela Earthquakes: Americans in need of consular assistance can call: +1-202-501-4444 (abroad) | +1-888-407-4747 (.., Canada) | Get Safety and Security Updates | Enroll in STEP | Follow: @TravelGov, @USEmbassyVE, WhatsApp “.. Citizens.” More information Worldwide Caution: The Department of State advises Americans worldwide to exercise increased caution. Advisory summary Crime Petty crime is common, especially in popular tourist spots. government employees working in Bolivia need special authorization to travel to Chapare Province due to a higher incidence of crimes connected to narcotrafficking. government employees working in Bolivia are encouraged to pay extra attention to their surroundings in El Alto because of petty crime. citizens in Bolivia are advised to take the same precautions. Check with your doctor about required vaccines and shots for high-risk exposure before you go to Bolivia. Consider the risk of traveling to high altitude locations in Bolivia.

excerpt: Safety and security - Bolivia travel advice - GOV.UK Cookies on GOV.UK We use some essential cookies to make this website work. Warning FCDO advises against all but essential travel to parts of Bolivia. Subscriptions Get email alerts Safety and security Terrorism There is a high threat of terrorist attack globally affecting UK interests and British nationals, including from groups and individuals who view the UK and British nationals as targets. Find out how to reduce your risk from terrorism while abroad . Crime Protecting your belongings Bolivia is experiencing an economic crisis and dollar shortage which has led to an increase in crime rates. Petty crime is common in central La Paz, in other popular tourist destinations, on buses and in crowded areas. On night buses, use a money belt under your clothes for important documents and credit cards; theft can occur when you are asleep. Violent crime There has been an increase in robberies from people taking taxis in Santa Cruz, La Paz and Rurrenabaque.

Bolivia - Exercise a high degree of caution Exercise a high degree of caution in Bolivia due to the continuing political and social tensions resulting in frequent roadblocks throughout the country.
